title: Worker attempt supervision
goal: Expose daemon-owned worker attempt supervision that distinguishes process liveness from worker output activity, warns on quiet workers without treating silence as failure by default, and keeps any fatal worker timeout explicit.
acceptance: Run progress includes structured worker attempt supervision for active slice and integration repair workers: attempt start, child PID when available, last child observation, last stdout/stderr/JSON worker event, semantic progress placeholder, configured attempt timeout, and quiet-output warning threshold.
Pi runner supervision updates are durable daemon state and survive outer CLI/Pi tool watcher timeouts; status/watch/monitor read the same state without owning run lifetime.
Monitor/watch output clearly separates supervisor liveness from worker output activity and can show quiet-worker warnings while saying silence may be normal.
Missing worker output is not failure by default; worker_attempt_timeout_seconds=0 disables fatal timeout and remains the default.
Any nonzero worker attempt timeout is explicit repo/operator policy, separate from global run lifetime, and timed-out attempts preserve normal retry/attempt history.
Cancellation/timeout termination attempts graceful stop before force kill using the configured worker termination grace period.
Tests cover a quiet Pi-compatible worker so status/monitor expose supervision fields and warning text before the run completes.
The optional Pi monitor overlay renders the same worker supervision fields from daemon status JSON when present.
must_ask_if: The implementation would add a hidden global workflow timeout.
The implementation would treat missing worker output as failure by default.
The implementation would auto-retry lost/stale work without preserving attempt history or at-least-once semantics.
